Item 2. Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

Net sales increased 8 percent driven by stronger-than-expected iPhone demand in the back-to-school season and a record quarterly performance from the Services segment across all geographic regions.

iPhone revenue declined 2 percent year-over-year as competitive intensity increased in China and carrier promotional activity moderated relative to the prior-year period.

Gross margin remained stable sequentially as pricing discipline and product mix offset modest increases in manufacturing and freight costs during the quarter.

We returned over 25 billion dollars to shareholders during the quarter through share repurchases and dividends, consistent with our long-standing capital return program and board authorization.

Apple Intelligence features launched in select markets and languages, contributing to strong upgrade intent among our installed base and supporting the long-term hardware upgrade cycle.

Item 3. Other Information
